message:
fix(doctor,reconstruct): preserve listener samples and cross-project canonical ids

Two distinct bugs reported by joyshmitz; both have the same shape (a kill-safe
or same-project guard being applied where weaker semantics are correct):

doctor CPU probe (#103):
  collect_doctor_server_runtime_diagnostics reused the kill-safe listener PID
  resolver for read-only CPU sampling. That resolver requires explicit Agent
  Mail signatures and refuses listener PIDs identified only via HTTP health.
  Result: doctor reports "No listener PID sample available" for live, verified
  Agent Mail servers whose process identity is not kill-safe. Adds
  doctor_listener_sample_pids and resolved_doctor_sample_pids: sample any
  listener PID once check_port_status returns AgentMailServer; reject Free /
  OtherProcess / Error. Kill semantics are unchanged.

reconstruct cross-project collision (#104):
  parse_and_insert_message dedup'd canonical message ids globally. Two project
  archives that independently coined frontmatter id=N would silently drop the
  second message. Now distinguishes:
    - same project_id + same canonical id  -> duplicate, skip (unchanged)
    - different project_id + same id       -> insert under generated DB id and
                                              record cross_project_canonical_collisions
                                              with a warning naming both project_ids
  message_project_id() backs the discrimination. Adds counter to ReconstructStats
  and a regression test that drives the full reconstruct pipeline with two
  project archives sharing id=7.

Tests: 6 new doctor sample selection tests + 1 cross-project reconstruct test.
All pass. Pre-existing same-project duplicate test still passes.
